btc_20pct_drop_probability.description: Based on the agents' discussions, posts, sentiment, concrete price predictions, on-chain reports, and any whale moves discussed in this branch, estimate the probability that BTC drops 20%+ from its current price ($20,500) within the next 24 hours of simulated time. Calibrate based on: consensus among agents (what fraction expect a drop vs hold); intensity of panic vs calm rhetoric; concrete BTC price targets mentioned ("BTC to $14k", "BTC sub-$17k by morning"); reports of institutional/whale selling; correlation arguments (e.g. "if SOL goes, BTC goes"). 0.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC holds; 1.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC will definitely break $16,400.

contagion_breadth_score.description: How wide is the contagion narrative? 0 = isolated to FTX/FTT/SOL only; 5 = mid-cap exchanges and lenders also dragged in; 10 = systemic crypto crisis discussion (BTC/ETH lows, stablecoin contagion, regulatory intervention talk). Score this branch's typical post.

ftx_withdrawal_halt_probability.description: Probability that FTX halts withdrawals within the next 24 simulated hours, inferred from agent discussions of withdrawal queue length, SBF statements, on-chain outflow signals, and prior-collapse comparisons.

panic_score.description: Average sentiment of branch posts about the cascade. 0 = total panic / "get out now" rhetoric; 5 = mixed / debated; 10 = calm / dismissive of contagion risk.

outcome_schema.description: Based on the agents' discussions, posts, sentiment, concrete price predictions, on-chain reports, and any whale moves discussed in this branch, estimate the probability that BTC drops 20%+ from its current price ($20,500) within the next 24 hours of simulated time. Calibrate based on: consensus among agents (what fraction expect a drop vs hold); intensity of panic vs calm rhetoric; concrete BTC price targets mentioned ("BTC to $14k", "BTC sub-$17k by morning"); reports of institutional/whale selling; correlation arguments (e.g. "if SOL goes, BTC goes"). 0.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC holds; 1.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC will definitely break $16,400.
outcome_schema.name: btc_20pct_drop_probability

outcome_schema.description: Probability that FTX halts withdrawals within the next 24 simulated hours, inferred from agent discussions of withdrawal queue length, SBF statements, on-chain outflow signals, and prior-collapse comparisons.

outcome_schema.description: How wide is the contagion narrative? 0 = isolated to FTX/FTT/SOL only; 5 = mid-cap exchanges and lenders also dragged in; 10 = systemic crypto crisis discussion (BTC/ETH lows, stablecoin contagion, regulatory intervention talk). Score this branch's typical post.

outcome_schema.description: Did 3+ posts discuss the possibility of Binance acquiring FTX?
outcome_schema.name: cz_acquisition_mentioned

outcome_schema.description: Did agents report SOL dropping 30%+ from its $32 starting price?
outcome_schema.name: sol_dump_observed

outcome_schema.description: Did agents express concern about USDT/USDC contagion from the FTX collapse (e.g. "Tether next?")?
outcome_schema.name: stablecoin_contagion_concern

outcome_schema.description: Did 2+ agents mention SEC/CFTC/DOJ likely involvement or specific regulator names as imminently relevant?
outcome_schema.name: regulator_action_anticipated

children.label: nested_institutional_bailout_stabilization
children.perturbation_text: A consortium led by BlackRock and Goldman Sachs has announced a $5 billion strategic partnership to acquire a majority stake in FTX, effectively absorbing all Alameda Research liabilities. The capital infusion is conditioned on a complete audit of all exchange reserves and the immediate resignation of the current FTX executive team. Market participants view this as a definitive 'too big to fail' intervention that guarantees the safety of all user deposits.

children.label: nested_systemic_collapse_escalation
children.perturbation_text: FTX officially suspended all user withdrawals at 3:00 AM ET, citing an unprecedented liquidity crunch and a failure to reconcile internal ledgers. Simultaneous reports from on-chain analysts reveal that Alameda Research has begun dumping significant portions of its SOL and ETH holdings to cover immediate margin calls. The total value locked in major DeFi lending protocols has plummeted by 40% as collateral liquidation triggers cascade across the ecosystem.

children.label: nested_regulatory_freeze_ambiguity
children.perturbation_text: The SEC and CFTC have issued a joint emergency order freezing all assets held by FTX, Alameda Research, and their affiliated offshore entities pending a formal investigation into potential securities violations. Regulators have stated that all withdrawals will remain paused indefinitely while they attempt to conduct a forensic accounting of the firm's balance sheet. Market participants are left in limbo, unable to determine if the exchange is insolvent or simply trapped in a temporary legal deadlock.

children.label: sbf_liquidity_injection
children.perturbation_text: Sam Bankman-Fried has issued a joint statement with major institutional partners announcing a $2 billion emergency liquidity injection into FTX. This capital is specifically earmarked to ensure full collateralization of all user withdrawal requests over the next 48 hours. FTT has surged 15% following the announcement.

children.label: binance_full_exit
children.perturbation_text: Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao has announced that the firm has completed the full liquidation of its FTT position and is now closing all operational ties with FTX. Simultaneously, major market makers have reported a total withdrawal of liquidity from FTX-linked order books. FTT has plummeted to $10, triggering a series of liquidations across DeFi lending protocols.

children.label: regulatory_inquiry_start
children.perturbation_text: The SEC and CFTC have released a joint statement confirming they have opened an inquiry into the financial relationship between Alameda Research and FTX. Regulators have requested detailed documentation regarding the usage of customer funds as collateral for trading activities. Markets are reacting with extreme caution as the scope of the investigation remains unclear.

children.label: solana_ecosystem_panic
children.perturbation_text: On-chain data confirms that Alameda Research has begun a massive, multi-million dollar liquidation of SOL tokens to address immediate margin calls. The Solana network is experiencing record-high transaction volume as retail holders rush to exit their positions. SOL has dropped 35% in value, dragging several prominent DeFi protocols into insolvency.

children.label: stablecoin_depeg_rumors
children.perturbation_text: Unverified reports are circulating on social media regarding the exposure of Tether and Circle to FTX-related credit facilities. While both firms maintain they have no direct exposure, USDC and USDT have begun to trade at a slight discount on secondary markets. Traders are moving aggressively into fiat-on-ramp-only assets as fears of a stablecoin depeg intensify.

children.label: binance_acquisition_talk
children.perturbation_text: Sources close to the negotiations suggest that Binance is in preliminary talks to acquire FTX's non-US assets to prevent a systemic collapse. Publicly, both CZ and SBF have declined to comment, fueling intense speculation about the terms of a potential bailout. FTT is experiencing extreme volatility as the market debates the likelihood of a rescue deal.

children.label: institutional_bank_run
children.perturbation_text: Major institutional hedge funds have publicly demanded immediate withdrawals of their tens of millions in assets from FTX. The exchange has disabled API withdrawal capabilities for institutional accounts, citing 'server maintenance.' Market sentiment has shifted to a state of panic as the reality of a full-scale bank run sets in.

children.label: market_wide_capitulation
children.perturbation_text: Following the breakdown of FTX withdrawal functionality, Bitcoin has broken through the $16,000 support level, triggering a massive wave of forced liquidations across the market. Global macro traders are citing the FTX contagion as the primary catalyst for a wider retreat from risk-on assets. ETH, SOL, and BTC are all down over 20% in the last 24 hours.
